---
title: "Common Critical Care Pharmacology"
---

Common Critical Care Pharmacy
Sedatives Dose Adverse Reactions Pearls
Propofol • Max 50 mcg/kg/min • Hypotension • Contraindications: Allergy to soy or egg
GABAareceptor • Apnea • Use with caution in patients with
agonist • Movement cardiovascular disease Rapid onset and
• Pain at the injection site offset
• Hypertriglyceridemia • Lipid vehicle is particularly susceptible to
• Pancreatitis bacterial contamination, change IV tubing
• Propofol-related infusion syndrome (PRIS): and bottle every 12 h
hyperkalemia, dysrhythmia, lipemia, metabolic • Blue-green discoloration of urine may occur
acidosis, heart failure, ±rhabdomyolysis; Risk • Propofol delivers 1.1 kcal/ml
increased in young people with high doses (>70
mcg/kg/min) for prolonged duration (>48 hrs)
Dexmedetomidine • Maintenance infusion: 0.2-1.4 • Hypotension • Has analgesic properties
Alpha-2 agonist mcg/kg/h • Transient hypotension, severe bradycardia, sinus • Goal is patient arousable with stimulation
• Loading dose (1 mcg/kg) NOT arrest during loading dose and sedative state when stimulation removed
recommended • Bradycardia • Does not require mechanical ventilation
Lorazepam • Loading dose: 0.02-0.04 mg/kg • Hypotension • IV contains propylene glycol, which can
GABAareceptor • Intermittent dose: 0.02-0.06 • Respiratory depression accumulate and cause metabolic acidosis
agonist mg/kg q2-6 h (max 2 mg IV for • Drowsiness • Precipitation is possible; an inline filter
agitation at 2 mg/min) • Pain at injection site recommended for infusions
• Maintenance infusion: 0.01-0.1 • Akathisia
mg/kg/h • Confusion
• Anterograde amnesia
• Visual disturbances
• Paradoxical reactions –hyperactivity and
aggressive behavior
Midazolam • Loading dose: 0.01-0.05 mg/kg • Similar tolorazepam • Rapid onset and short duration
GABAareceptor • Maintenance infusion: 0.01-0.1 • Active metabolites which may accumulate in
agonist mg/kg/h renal dysfunction
Opioids Usual dose Adverse Reactions Pearls
Fentanyl • Bolus: 50-100 mcg or 1-2 • Respiratory • Constipation • May be alternative in patients with morphine
mcg/kg IVP depression • Miosis allergy
• Maintenance: 0.7-10 mcg/kg/hr • Bradycardia • Chest wall rigidity • Do not use transdermal formulation in acute
or 25-700 mcg/h • Edema • Diaphoresis setting due to delayed onset (6–24 h)
• Confusion • Myoclonus
• Sedation
• Mood changes
Hydromorphone • Oral: 2-4 mg every 4-6 h • Respiratory • Mood changes • May be alternative in patients with morphine
• IV: 0.2-1 mg every 4-6 h depression • Constipation allergy
• Bradycardia • Miosis
• Edema • Diaphoresis
• Confusion • Myoclonus
• Sedation
Morphine • Bolus: 2-10 mg IVP • Itching • Pruritus • Active metabolites may accumulate in renal
• Intermediate dose: 2-8 mg q3-4 • Respiratory • Urinary retention dysfunction; reduced dose recommended
h depression • Pain at injection site • Can induce a histamine release that causes
• Maintenance infusion: 0.8-30 • Sedation • Dizziness itching
mg/h • Hypotension • Fever
• Pediatric: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg IV over • Mood changes • Myoclonus
5 min; maintenance infusion • Xerostomia • May elevate ICP due
0.02-0.03 mg/kg/h, titrate as • Constipation to hypercarbia
needed
Naloxone • IV/IM: 0.04-0.4 mg • Tachycardia • Diaphoresis • Due to short half-life, effects may wear off
(antagonist) • Intranasal: 1-2 mg per nareinto • Hypertension • Dyspnea before opioid is cleared from the system,
both nares, can be repeated q2-3 • Nausea, vomiting • Withdrawal resulting in renarcotization
min and switch to IV/IM when • Tremor symptoms
possible
Neuromuscular Dosing Onset Duration Pearls Adverse Effects
Blocking Agent
Succinylcholine Adults Very Rapid Short • Cannot be reversed • Hypersensitivit
• IV 0.5 to 1.1 mg/kg • IV: 30–60 s Average 3–5 min • May cause slight increases in ICP y reactions,
• IM 2–4 mg/kg • IM: 4 mins Max 7–10 min • Severe hyperkalemia may occur if burns, including
Adolescent: 1 mg/kg IV severe muscle trauma, neuromuscular disease, anaphylaxis
Pediatrics: 2 mg/kg IV stroke, SCI, MS, prolonged immobilization • Cardiac arrest
• Contraindicated in malignant hyperthermia • Cardiac
• Hyperkalemia arrhythmias
Vecuronium Adults and pediatrics: 0.1 Prolonged Intermediate • No significant cardiovascular effects • Malignant
mg/kg (up to 0.2 mg/kg) 3-9 mins 35-45 mins (up to 60 • No effect on ICP hyperthermia
IV Faster onset mins) • Hypertension
with higher dose or hypotension
(2 min) • Hyperkalemia
Cisatracurium Adults and pediatrics: Fast Prolonged • Longer half life in elderly • Prolonged
0.15 mg/kg (up to 0.2 1.5-2 mins Adults: 45-75 mins • Elimination via enzymatic breakdown and respiratory
mg/kg) IV Pediatrics: 20-35 does not rely on renal or liver function for depression
mins clearance • Jaw rigidity
• Rhabdomyolysi
Rocuronium Adults: 0.6 mg/kg (up to Rapid Intermediate • Prolonged duration in renal failure s
1.2 mg/kg) IV 1-2 mins 20-35 mins (up to 60 • Myalgias
Pediatrics: 0.45-0.6 Faster onset mins) • Skeletal muscle
mg/kg IV with higher dose weakness
